goal: For 6 pick targets on a fixture, report reachability, joint-limit margin, and fixture clearance for a UR10e+EOAT.
thoughts: solve_ik returns ONE iterative solution -- be explicit that the wrist-3 branch can pick a near-limit pose when a free-of-limit solution exists. Always pair reachability with a clearance check; 'reachable' without collision verification is a common L-level fail. Output a table keyed on target index.
code:
targets = ["<T1>", "<T2>", "<T3>", "<T4>", "<T5>", "<T6>"]
joint_limits = get_joint_limits(articulation_path="/World/UR10e")
report = []
for idx, tgt in enumerate(targets):
    ik = solve_ik(
        articulation_path="/World/UR10e",
        end_effector_path="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT/tip",
        target_pose=tgt,
        max_iterations=200,
        position_tolerance_mm=0.5
    )
    if not ik.success:
        report.append({"target": idx, "reachable": False})
        continue
    margins = [
        min(q - lo, hi - q)
        for q, (lo, hi) in zip(ik.joint_positions, joint_limits.limits_rad)
    ]
    clear = check_collisions(
        subjects=["/World/UR10e"],
        targets=["/World/Fixture"],
        ignore_self=True
    )
    sweep = sweep_sphere(
        start="<APPROACH>", end=tgt, radius="<R_MM>",
        ignore_prims=["/World/UR10e"]
    )
    report.append({
        "target": idx,
        "reachable": True,
        "min_joint_margin_rad": min(margins),
        "min_clearance_mm": min(clear.min_distance_mm, sweep.min_clearance_mm)
    })
failure_modes: Reporting 'reachable' without running the clearance check
Claiming IK found 'the' solution when multiple branches exist (wrist-3 near-limit branch)
Confusing UR10e's 1300mm datasheet reach (center of flange) with tool-tip reach after EOAT extension
